results.title: The userGaugeProfitIndex is not set correctly, allowing an attacker to receive rewards without waiting
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit item 28 describes the same issue in ProfitManager.claimGaugeRewards(): when user gauge weight is zero, the function returns early without synchronizing userGaugeProfitIndex. GuildToken.incrementGauge calls claimGaugeRewards before increasing weight, leaving the user’s index at 0 and allowing them to claim historical rewards after adding weight. This matches the vulnerability description’s mechanism, code path, and fix (initialize/synchronize userGaugeProfitIndex to the current gaugeProfitIndex when weight is zero or index is 0). Fixing the audit’s issue would fix the described vulnerability.

results.title: Anyone can steal all distributed rewards
results.reasoning: Yes. Finding #25 explicitly reports “ERC20RebaseDistributor Self-Transfer Allows Rebasing User to Steal Unminted Rebase Rewards,” which is the same issue as the described [H-02] bug: a self-transfer while rebasing lets a user mint/steal all unminted rebase rewards due to caching/state misuse in the transfer path. Both target ERC20RebaseDistributor’s transfer/self-transfer logic, enable theft of all unminted rewards, and would be fixed by preventing self-transfers or correcting the caching/state update. Although code references in #25 are left undefined, the vulnerability mechanism, code path, and impact match exactly.
